stories.completionNotes: Root cause: orchestrator had NO automatic sync trigger. Spool hypothesis (c) 'orchestrator wiring dropped during refactor' was closest but the truer framing is 'never wired to begin with' -- US-154 shipped scripts/sync_now.py as the manual Walk-phase trigger with a comment 'auto-scheduling is Run-phase scope', and that Run-phase auto-trigger was never shipped. The HomeNetworkDetector (US-188) exists but is not wired into the orchestrator anywhere. The only existing SyncClient instantiation in the orchestrator was US-225's lazy _forceSyncPush, which only fires on a PowerDownOrchestrator WARNING stage (requires UpsMonitor). Hypotheses (a) 'config key rename' and (b) 'drive_end-bound trigger' both ruled out: (a) pi.companionService.* are present + consumed correctly by SyncClient.__init__; (b) no drive_end-bound sync path existed at all in Sprint 17 (not just broken -- missing). Last-sync timestamp 2026-04-19T13:48:05Z is CIO/Ralph running sync_now.py manually before Session 23 UPS drain test, matches Session 6 timeline. Fix: new pi.sync.* section governs WHEN (trigger policy) distinct from pi.companionService.* which governs HOW (transport); _initializeSyncClient in lifecycle.py constructs the client respecting pi.sync.enabled + swallowing missing-API-key as warning (does not crash boot); ApplicationOrchestrator gains _syncClient attribute + _maybeTriggerIntervalSync method called once per runLoop pass (cheap cadence gate) + triggerDriveEndSync hooked into _handleDriveEnd; interval path fires independently of drive_end (invariant per US-229 bug). Flush-on-boot behavior (first runLoop pass triggers push) ensures Drive 3's stranded rows land immediately on next boot. Integration test proves orchestrator-level sync trigger pushes pending rows end-to-end to mock server. filesActuallyTouched: config.json (new pi.sync section) + src/common/config/validator.py (3 DEFAULTS + _validatePiSync method + top-level _validatePiSync call + mod-history) + src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (new _initializeSyncClient + _shutdownSyncClient + ordering in _initializeAllComponents + _shutdownAllComponents + _syncClient type hint + 2 mod-history entries) + src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py (new _syncClient + _lastSyncAttemptTime + _syncIntervalSeconds + _syncTriggerOn attributes + syncClient property + _maybeTriggerIntervalSync + triggerDriveEndSync methods + runLoop wiring + 1 mod-history entry) + src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/event_router.py (triggerDriveEndSync hook in _handleDriveEnd) + specs/architecture.md (US-226 Sync trigger semantics + recovery playbook subsection) + docs/testing.md (Pi->Server Sync Recovery section + mod-history entry) + tests/pi/sync/test_client_config_paths.py (new, 6 tests) + tests/pi/orchestrator/test_sync_wiring.py (new, 17 tests) + tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_sync_recovery.py (new, 4 tests). Verification: 27 new tests all pass in 98s; ruff --fix clean on all 8 touched files (import-sort only); validate_config OK; sprint_lint 0 errors / 23 pre-existing sizing warnings. Deliberate design choices: interval cadence MANDATORY when sync enabled (validator enforces it -- defensive against the US-229 drive_end bug). First runLoop pass triggers immediate push so Drive 3's stranded rows land on boot. Drive-end trigger resets _lastSyncAttemptTime so a recently-ended drive doesn't double-push on next interval tick. Transport failures don't crash runLoop (broad-exception capture in both trigger paths) + preserve HWM (inherited pushDelta invariant). Also hit a grounding gap worth flagging: story's spec text repeatedly mentions 'pi.sync section' as if it exists or was renamed, but the actual production config uses pi.companionService (for transport) -- my fix treats pi.sync as a NEW section for trigger policy rather than renaming/removing pi.companionService, which preserves US-149/US-151/US-225 behavior. Real-world verification deferred to CIO/Ralph running deploy-pi.sh + observing 'Interval sync:' log lines in journalctl. Drive 3's 3272 rows will land on chi-srv-01 on next deploy + boot (flush-on-boot behavior). Pi DB + server DB pre/post-verification commands in docs/testing.md recovery playbook.

stories.title: Fix US-206 drive_summary NULL on cold-start -- timing bug drive_start before first IAT reading
stories.intent: Drive 3 (2026-04-23) shipped a drive_summary row with NULL for all three sensor fields (ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, barometric_kpa_at_start) even though those values ARE present in realtime_data for the same drive. Root cause: drive_start fires before first IAT/BATTERY_V/BARO readings arrive from ECU; the SummaryRecorder captures the snapshot early, sees no values, writes NULL, and doesn't backfill. Bug breaks US-206's whole purpose -- ambient/baro/battery context per drive is the anchor for Spool's interpretation layer. Two fix options: (a) defer drive_summary INSERT until first IAT reading captured, (b) UPDATE the row when first matching readings arrive post-INSERT. Ralph picks + tests.

stories.completionNotes: Chosen strategy: Option (b) backfill-UPDATE. Rationale: Option (a) defer-INSERT would break the invariant 'drive_summary row MUST exist for every drive_id' unless it added a timeout-fallback INSERT -- which is basically Option (b) plus more moving parts. Option (b) keeps the drive_start INSERT unconditional (row always exists), then UPDATEs still-NULL sensor columns from each processValue tick's snapshot during a bounded window. Implementation: (1) new SummaryRecorder.backfillFromSnapshot(driveId, snapshot, fromState) -> SummaryBackfillResult with COALESCE semantics (never overwrites a non-NULL stored value; warm-restart ambient rule auto-preserved because buildSummaryFromSnapshot already returns ambient=None when fromState not in {UNKNOWN, KEY_OFF}). (2) DetectorConfig gains driveSummaryBackfillSeconds (default 60s via new DEFAULT_DRIVE_SUMMARY_BACKFILL_SECONDS constant). (3) DriveDetector tracks _driveSummaryBackfillDriveId / _driveSummaryBackfillDeadline / _driveSummaryBackfillComplete / _driveSummaryBackfillFromState, arms via _armDriveSummaryBackfill(startTime) inside _startDrive right after _captureDriveStartSummary, disarms at _endDrive. (4) processValue tick calls _maybeBackfillDriveSummary(now) which early-exits when complete / past deadline / not RUNNING / no recorder / no snapshot source / empty snapshot, else dispatches backfillFromSnapshot; flips complete=True when result.complete. (5) 'complete' semantics: cold start needs all three sensor cols non-NULL; warm restart needs only battery + baro (ambient is not applicable by design). Pre-flight bug reproduced: ssh/test showed empty snapshot at drive_start writes (None, None, None); additionally surfaced a latent risk that a later partial snapshot via the existing captureDriveStart UPSERT could clobber already-captured non-NULL cols with NULL -- the new backfillFromSnapshot method uses COALESCE-style logic that prevents that. Scope note: story's filesToTouch listed 7 files; I touched 7 matching items but ALSO src/pi/obdii/drive/types.py to add the DetectorConfig field + default constant. Types.py is the natural home for DetectorConfig + its defaults; adding a config-controlled backfill window without hardcoding requires the field live there. Flagging as minor scope bleed that's structurally inseparable from the detector.py change. Did NOT touch src/pi/obdii/data/logger.py (getLatestReadings already exposes the snapshot shape the backfill needs; no new hook required) -- confirmed per the filesToTouch note 'confirm snapshot shape exposes IAT/BATTERY_V/BARO or expose hook'; confirmation, not change. Added driveSummaryBackfillSeconds=60 to config.json pi.analysis section for explicit discoverability (alongside existing driveStartRpmThreshold / driveStartDurationSeconds / driveEndRpmThreshold / driveEndDurationSeconds). Verification: 13 new tests pass (8 unit + 5 integration); full fast suite 3212 passed / 17 skipped / 0 regressions (baseline post-US-226 was 3199 -- exactly +13 matches the new tests); ruff clean on all 5 touched files; validate_config OK post config.json change; sprint_lint 0 errors / 23 pre-existing sizing warnings (same baseline as US-226 session). Operator fallback: bump pi.analysis.driveSummaryBackfillSeconds in config.json if the ECU is slow; check journalctl for 'drive_summary backfill window expired' entries. Invariants honored: (1) drive_summary row per drive_id still INSERT'd at drive_start regardless of snapshot contents; (2) warm-restart ambient stays NULL even if IAT arrives during the backfill window (covered by test_warmRestartBackfillSkipsAmbient and test_warmRestartBackfillDoesNotFillAmbient); (3) bounded window via DetectorConfig; (4) no new PID polls (backfill consumes ObdDataLogger.getLatestReadings snapshot only -- same read-only API US-206 uses).

stories.title: Fix drive_end not firing -- ELM_VOLTAGE heartbeat keeps drive open past engine-off
stories.intent: Drive 3 engine-off at 16:46:21 UTC but NO drive_end event ever fired in connection_log. Drive_detector keys on 'rows still arriving' to infer engine state -- but ELM_VOLTAGE (adapter-level PID, not ECU-dependent) kept ticking for 6+ minutes after engine-off, so the detector thought the drive was still active. Drive 3 is 'effectively still open right now' per Spool. Compounds US-226 if sync is drive_end-bound -- but US-226 will add interval-bound sync as defensive measure, so this bug's downstream impact is the ANALYTICS trigger (per-drive stats computed at drive_end). Fix: filter drive-end detection on ECU-sourced PIDs only; ignore ELM_VOLTAGE and any other adapter-level heartbeat.

stories.title: Journald persistence deploy hardening -- verify /var/log/journal populated post-deploy
stories.completionNotes: Session 101, Rex (Agent 1). Strengthened deploy/deploy-pi.sh::step_install_journald_persistent from 'does /var/log/journal exist?' (weaker check that missed Spool's 2026-04-23 failure mode) to 'does /var/log/journal/<machine-id>/ exist AND journalctl --disk-usage report >0 bytes?' (the actual persistence signal). Reads /etc/machine-id on the Pi, derives the expected subdir path, verifies both signals; on failure emits the 5 US-230 AC #3 diagnostics (disk-usage / ls / --verify / conf.d contents / is-active) via a local emit_journald_diagnostics helper defined inside the remote heredoc, then exits 7. The post-check runs UNCONDITIONALLY on every deploy (not just when the drop-in content changed) because Spool's failure mode was 'already-installed drop-in on an empty /var/log/journal' -- a skip-when-unchanged post-check would have missed it. New 2s sleep after any actual restart (only when content changed) + 3s retry on zero disk-usage cover first-run + slow-disk edge cases. Per invariant #2: no mkdir as silent recovery. Added tests/deploy/test_journald_persistent_install.sh (4 assertions: machine-id non-empty, subdir exists, disk-usage positive, journald active; exits 77 autotools-SKIP when SSH to Pi unreachable so CI runners without Pi access don't red-flag). Scope-fence honorarium flagged in Spool inbox for Marcus to adjudicate. docs/testing.md gained 'Verifying persistent journald after deploy' section with 4 subsections (via deploy script / bash test / SSH spot-check / off-Pi pytest wrapper). stories.intent: US-210 shipped /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/99-obd-persistent.conf with Storage=persistent, but Spool's post-deploy audit found /var/log/journal/ EMPTY on the Pi. Systemd-journald never picked up the new config -- either the restart didn't fire in the deploy step, failed silently, or journal is being written to /run/log/journal (tmpfs) still. Fix: deploy-pi.sh step_install_journald_persistent adds a post-check that verifies /var/log/journal/<machine-id>/ exists after the systemctl restart systemd-journald call. If missing, fail deploy loudly instead of proceeding silently. Also add acceptance test that SSH-verifies persistent journal after a fresh deploy. Downstream impact: next Pi crash without persistent journal = log evidence gone (the exact pattern Session 6 UPS drain hit); also blocks US-216 orchestrator forensic testing.

stories.title: Server tier systemd unit -- mirror of US-210 for chi-srv-01
stories.intent: Chi-Srv-01 runs uvicorn src.server.main:app as a user-mode process under mcornelison (PID 3985160 per Spool's audit). No obd-server.service systemd unit. No Restart policy. No autostart on reboot. Any server reboot or process crash = server stays down until CIO manually restarts it; Pi sync fails silently post-reboot. This is the server-tier parallel of pre-US-210 Pi state. Fix: create + install deploy/obd-server.service with Restart=always + RestartSec=5 + After=network.target mariadb.service + User=mcornelison + EnvironmentFile for DB creds. Deploy step in deploy/deploy-server.sh installs + enables the unit. One-time cutover migrates currently-running uvicorn cleanly. Acceptance: server restarts on process-kill + on host-reboot.

stories.title: TD-035 close -- retry loop responds to SIGTERM within 2s
stories.intent: Close TD-035 (filed 2026-04-23 Sprint 17 post-deploy debug). systemctl restart eclipse-obd takes ~90s during car-off because the collector's BT retry loop uses time.sleep(backoff) which doesn't wake on SIGTERM. Systemd ends up SIGKILLing after TimeoutStopSec. Service ends up in failed state requiring systemctl reset-failed. Bigger downstream risk: if US-216's TRIGGER stage sends SIGTERM for graceful shutdown and collector is in retry sleep, the 90s wait may overlap with actual power loss. Fix: replace time.sleep with threading.Event.wait + SIGTERM handler sets the event. Symmetric with how US-211 ReconnectLoop should also handle shutdown signals.

stories.completionNotes: Session 102 (Rex) -- TD-035 closed Option 1 (threading.Event.wait in place of time.sleep). ObdConnection + ReconnectLoop + buildDefaultReconnectLoop each gain an optional shutdownEvent seam; orchestrator core.py creates a shared threading.Event in __init__ (self._shutdownEvent), signal_handler.py sets it on SIGTERM/SIGINT alongside ShutdownState.SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED, lifecycle.py plumbs it into createConnectionFromConfig, bt_resilience.py plumbs it into buildDefaultReconnectLoop. Backwards-compat preserved: when no event is passed (legacy callers / tests) the retry loops fall back to time.sleep and behavior is unchanged (test_connectRetryLoop_noShutdownEvent_preservesTimeSleep + test_reconnectLoop_noShutdownEvent_preservesExistingBehavior guard this). 10 new tests total (4 in test_sigterm_responsiveness.py, 6 in test_reconnect_loop_sigterm.py) verify: (1) event set mid-sleep wakes within <3s against a 60s-cap backoff; (2) pre-set event skips all backoffs instantly; (3) shutdownEvent composes with shouldExitFn without regressing the US-211 abort path; (4) buildDefaultReconnectLoop factory accepts + plumbs the event; (5) event set mid-retry stops the next attempt from dispatching. Backoff schedule invariant preserved (no changes to [1,5,10,30,60]); one-shot event invariant preserved (never cleared once set). main.py required NO changes -- signal handlers are owned by the orchestrator mixin. stopConditions cleared: signal handlers already installed on main thread (orchestrator SignalHandlerMixin); ReconnectLoop's sleep is in the orchestrator's synchronous capture-error path, no sub-thread work needed; real-world restart <10s test deferred to CIO deploy (no regression surface here -- the event-wait primitive is the stdlib idiom referenced in TD-035 Option 1, tested deterministically with a helper thread). A separate main.py handler would fight the orchestrator for signal ownership and break the double-signal force-exit pattern. stories.title: Pre-mint orphan-row backfill -- 225 Drive 3 rows with NULL drive_id from BT-connect window
stories.intent: Drive 3 has 225 rows tagged data_source='real' with NULL drive_id from the 39-second BT-connect-to-drive-start window. The collector was connected to the car and writing rows, but the engine-state machine hadn't yet fired the CRANKING transition that mints the drive_id. Drive 3's drive_id was minted only after engine started. Similar to Session 23's pre-US-200 NULL-drive_id issue but narrower (39s window vs 2+ min). Ralph shipped a backfill for the older data in Sprint 15; reuse that pattern + apply to Drive 3's pre-mint orphans OR extend the engine_state machine to cover the BT-connect-before-cranking window with a distinct drive_id (or NULL drive_id by policy, documented). Low urgency -- doesn't block anything, just keeps drive-scoped queries tidy.

src/pi/obdii/engine_state.py (decide policy: (a) backfill via script after-the-fact, (b) pre-emit a provisional drive_id at BT-connect that gets reconciled at CRANKING, (c) leave NULL and document as authoritative policy)

BT-connect fires before CRANKING transition; drive_id minted at CRANKING only
Three options: (a) backfill script, (b) provisional drive_id at BT-connect, (c) document NULL as policy

Policy decision documented in completionNotes: (a), (b), or (c) with rationale.
If (a) backfill script: scripts/backfill_premint_orphans.py implements the match logic + idempotent + dry-run gate.
If (b) provisional drive_id: engine_state.py + drive_id.py updated to mint provisional ID at BT-connect that gets reconciled at CRANKING (with policy for how to handle mid-window disconnect).

invariants: If backfill is the chosen strategy, it MUST be idempotent. Re-running on already-backfilled data is a no-op.

Do NOT retroactively change drive_summary row ownership (the drive_summary's drive_id is authoritative).
Do NOT use this script/policy to retrofit Session 23's 149 rows -- those are the regression fixture and stay frozen.

Reasoning in offices/tuner/inbox/2026-04-23-from-ralph-us233-premint-orphan-backfill.md: (b) risks breaking US-219 review ritual + US-214 dual-writer reconciliation per stopConditions[2,3]; (c) leaves Spool unable to include the 39 pre-cranking seconds in his per-drive analysis. New scripts/backfill_premint_orphans.py (~380 LOC, public API: scanOrphans / scanDriveStarts / findOrphanBackfillMatches / applyBackfill / renderReport / backupDatabase / main; dataclasses OrphanRow / DriveStart / BackfillMatch; SafetyCapError + BackfillError exceptions). CLI: --db PATH required, --dry-run / --execute mutex, --window-seconds N (default 60), --max-orphans-per-drive N (default 1000), --sentinel-dir DIR.
